I bring a rare combination of project management, business intelligence, and data analytics

— with 4+ years of combined academic and professional experience supporting organizations through data-driven transformation, operational improvement, and strategic decision-making across healthcare, financial services, retail distribution, and the public sector.

Selected Achievements

  • Built 10+ interactive dashboards and analytical reports across professional and academic settings using Power BI, Adobe Analytics, Tableau, and Excel — including a customer churn driver analysis, NYPD demographic correlation analysis across 9.4M records, data science job market insights, Boston crime temporal analysis, and 2 executive KPI dashboards at Fidelity Investments tracking acquisition, engagement, retention, and attrition.
  • Identified and escalated 3 critical data quality errors at Fidelity Investments — including misattributed landing page traffic in Adobe Analytics that would have corrupted month-over-month and year-over-year business reporting across downstream executive dashboards.
  • Led distribution route optimization analysis across 500,000+ retail points at Orange Drug, projecting a 20% reduction in fuel costs and 30% fewer repeat visits; drove product penetration into 8 new states through regional pricing and consumer behavior analysis.
  • Supported rollout of the ORBIT demand-driven ordering system across 36 states at Orange Group — coordinating store location mapping and distribution of point-of-sale systems to transform inventory management from a fixed-schedule model to a real-time demand-based system.
  • Digitized end-to-end operations at Rosa Couture — transitioning procurement, production tracking, and fulfillment workflows from paper-based systems to structured digital reporting, improving fulfillment accuracy by 25% and reducing delivery delays by 20%.

Portfolio

Projects

Power BI  ·  Workforce Analytics  ·  Live Interactive Dashboard

Data Science Job Insights Dashboard

Interactive Power BI dashboard exploring the evolving dynamics of the technology industry workforce — hiring volumes, skill demand, salary distributions, and experience requirements across major tech job categories.

  • Tracks hiring volumes across Software Engineering, Data Science, Product Management, and Cybersecurity.
  • Highlights most sought-after skills including Python, SQL, Cloud Computing, and Machine Learning.
  • Compares compensation across roles by job type, experience level, and specialization.
  • Interactive filters allow drill-down by job type, region, or experience range.
View Full Dashboard →
Power BI  ·  Crime Analytics

NYPD Crime Analytics Dashboard

A demographic correlation and gap analysis dashboard built on 9,470,313 NYPD complaint records. The core question: are there statistically meaningful correlations between the demographics of victims and suspects — by race, age group, and gender — and do those patterns reveal targeting behavior across demographic lines? A secondary gap analysis examined how many complaints resulted in arrests, surfacing systemic gaps in case resolution across boroughs and offense types.

  • Correlation analysis between victim race and suspect race to identify whether particular racial groups were disproportionately targeted by specific suspect demographics.
  • Cross-demographic breakdowns of victim age group vs. suspect age group and victim race vs. victim sex to surface intersectional crime patterns.
  • Gap analysis tracking complaint-to-arrest conversion rates across boroughs, offense categories, and demographic segments.
  • Time-series trend analysis and district-level crime breakdowns with interactive filters for borough, offense type, and complaint date.
View Full Dashboard →

Boston Crime Analysis Dashboard

A multi-view Tableau dashboard analyzing 358,304 Boston crime incidents from 2017 to 2020. The project examines when, where, and what types of crimes occur — combining a high-level summary view with a deep temporal analysis to surface patterns across years, months, days of the week, and hours of the day. Incident counts declined consistently over the period, with a notable 18.68% drop in 2020. The dashboard is built to help public safety stakeholders understand seasonal trends, peak crime periods, and district-level patterns to inform resource allocation and prevention strategies.

  • Year-over-year trend analysis from 101,338 incidents in 2017 to 70,894 in 2020, with percentage change tracking across all years.
  • Temporal breakdowns by day of week, hour of day, and day of month — revealing Friday as the peak day and afternoon/evening hours as highest-risk periods.
  • District-level crime trends and monthly patterns showing January, March, and August as peak months and December as the lowest.
  • Top incident categories ranked: Motor Vehicle Accidents, Larceny, Medical Assistance, Investigate Person, and Simple Assault.
View Temporal Analysis → View Summary Dashboard →
Power BI  ·  Customer Analytics  ·  Ad Hoc Analysis

Customer Churn Analysis Dashboard

An ad hoc analytical deep-dive into the drivers of customer churn. Rather than simply tracking churn rates, this dashboard was built to answer a specific business question: why are customers leaving? The analysis examines churn across demographic segments, contract types, service usage patterns, and tenure to identify the characteristics most strongly associated with attrition and surface actionable retention insights.

  • Churn driver analysis across key variables including contract type, payment method, tenure, and service subscriptions to isolate the strongest predictors of attrition.
  • Demographic segmentation of churned customers by age group, gender, and account characteristics to identify high-risk customer profiles.
  • Service usage breakdown comparing churned vs. retained customers across features such as internet service, tech support, and online security.
  • Interactive filters enabling ad hoc exploration of churn patterns by customer segment, service type, and contract length.
View Full Dashboard →
